title: Jazz à Ramatuelle
subTitle: China Moses takes the stage at Ramatuelle in 2016.

title: The Paris Reunion Band
subTitle: The Paris Reunion Band, formed as homage to Kenny Clarke and the spirit of his fellow Paris jazzmen, recreates the sound of Paris in the 1950s and 60s when it was Europe's mecca for American jazz musicians.

title: Modern Jazz Quartet at the Bozar
subTitle: The Modern Jazz Quartet (MJQ) was composed of Milt 'Bags' Jackson on vibraphone, John Lewis (piano), Percy Heath (bass), and Connie Kay (drums). Jackson and Lewis were pioneer bebop musicians who had played together in Dizzy Gillespie's big band.

title: Jamie Cullum: Jazz à Vienne 2017
subTitle: Watch the performance of Jamie Cullum live at Jazz à Vienne, in 2017.

title: Jazzmeia Horn at Malta Jazz Festival
subTitle: Vocalist Jazzmeia Horn's 2019 Malta Jazz Festival appearance; a celebration of jazz's future, infused with the soul of its past.

title: Christian McBride Quartet at Malta 2018
subTitle: Double bassist Christian McBride presents his band New Jawn at the Malta Jazz Festival on July 20, 2018; hailing from Philadelphia, this musician combines jazz, R&B, pop/rock, hip hop/neo-soul, and classical.

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1987, prominent guitarist George Benson brought his band to kick off day three of the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague; joining him to play songs from their joint album Collaborations was acoustic guitarist Earl Klugh.

title: James Brown : The North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1981, the legendary American singer James Brown gave an unforgettable performance to the audience at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague, the Netherlands.

title: Charles Mingus and Eric Dolphy Live in Liège
subTitle: Charles Mingus showcases a concert performed in April 1965 featuring Jaki Byard on piano, Dannie Richmond on drums, Johnny Coles on trumpet, Clifford Jordan on tenor sax and Eric Dolphy on alto sax, flute and bass clarinet.

title: Lianne La Havas: Live at Casino de Paris
subTitle: Lianne La Havas' performance at Casino de Paris shows the British singer-songwriter at her best; this concert is part of La Havas' "Blood Tour", promoting her second album "Blood."

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1985, American jazz bassist and composer Charlie Haden brings his own New Liberation Orchestra to the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague for a performance.

title: Chicken
subTitle: The audience at the Montreal Jazz Festival in Canada was in for a treat on July 3, 1982, as a musical innovator hit the stage; Jaco Pastorius transforms the electric bass into a solo instrument.

subTitle: Bill Evans: Live in '64, '65 & '70 features five separate performances by one of the most revered pianists in music history.

title: Stingray Djazz
subTitle: In 2019, the city of Sofia, Bulgaria, hosts the International A to JazZ Festival; among the featured acts is the jazz trumpeter Christian Scott aTunde Adjuah.

subTitle: Featuring three concerts filmed in Holland, Switzerland, and Belgium that highlight the bebop musician's classic style and silky tone.

subTitle: Featuring a blistering one-hour concert from 1964, showcasing one of the hot line-ups of the Swinging Herd.

subTitle: In 1979, legendary American blues guitarist B.B. King gave an unforgettable performance at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague, Netherlands.

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1986, multi-talented American guitarist John Scofield and his band performed a concert at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague; first known for playing with Miles Davis, Scofield shows here that he earned that pedigree.

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: Back in 1985, legendary American trumpeter Miles Davis gave an unforgettable performance at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague. Davis was one of the most influential jazz musicians ever.

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1986, Cuban trumpeter Arturo Sandoval brought his band with him to the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague. Featuring talented performers like pianist Hilario Durán and guitarist Jorge Chicoy, they displayed a master class in Latin jazz fusion.

subTitle: Performances of pianist Joe Turner, the Kenny Clarke Trio and the Art Simmons Trio.

title: Münchner Klaviersommer
subTitle: At Münchner Klaviersommer 1994, Cuban pianist Gonzalo Rubalcaba performed a trio concert with Ron Carter on bass and Julio Barreto on drums. The music demonstrates Rubalcaba's brilliant improvisational and technical abilities.

title: Naima
subTitle: The collection features John Coltrane's performances from the early 1960s, showcasing his influential saxophone artistry.

title: Seun Kuti & Egypt 80'
subTitle: Seun Anikulapo Kuti possesses the grace, energy and fury of his father, the legendary artist Fela Kuti; with the musicians of Fela Kuti's legendary band Egypt 80, Seaun Kuti brings to life the most original incarnation of Afrobeat.

subTitle: Pianist Kenny Barron's appearance at the Alfa Jazz Festival in Ukraine's Lviv is a celebration of jazz virtuosity.

title: Donna Lee
subTitle: The audience at the Montreal Jazz Festival in Canada was in for a treat on July 3, 1982, as a true musical innovator hit the stage; a look at Jaco Pastorius, who transformed the electric bass into a formidable solo instrument.

title: Jazz Samba: Baden Powell, Ernesto Ribeiro
subTitle: Recorded in 1971 in Paris, Brazilian, classically trained bossa nova guitarist Baden Powell and his quartet play various compositions, including some originals.

title: Stingray Djazz
subTitle: Chucho Valdes and his Afro-Cuban messengers are the prime example of Latin-inspired jazz music; during his performance the pianist brings the streets of Havana to the Jazz a Vienne stage.

title: Fine and Mellow

title: Münchner Klaviersommer: Eliane Elias Trio

title: Dizzy Gillespie - Jazz Greats
subTitle: American jazz trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie was an influential figure of bebop known for fusing Cuban, Latin American, and Carribean influences in his music.

title: Live at Estival Lugano - Franco Ambrosetti, George Gruntz, Steve Coleman & J.F. Clark
subTitle: Since 1977, Estival is a summer jazz festival in Lugano, Switzerland; Estival offers a thrilling and particularly surprising line-up that explores the rich world of contemporary music whilst promoting tolerance, and co-existence.

title: Miriam Makeba Live at Estival Lugano 1985
subTitle: Miriam Makeba, also named Mama Africa, was a South African singer, actress, United Nations goodwill ambassador, and civil-rights activist; she brought African music to a Western audience, and popularized the world music and Afropop genres.

subTitle: Pianist Kenny Barron's appearance at the Alfa Jazz Festival in Ukraine's Lviv is a celebration of jazz virtuosity.

title: The Great Charlie Galbraith All Star Jazz Band
subTitle: In 1963, the small Belgian town of Comblain-la-Tour hosted the talented groups of Charlie Galbraith, Knut Kiesewetter, Bud Shank and Pim Jacobs and Marc Laferrière in a jazz festival that mixed new and old styles.

subTitle: In 2019, the city of Sofia, Bulgaria, hosts the International A to JazZ Festival; among the featured acts is the jazz trumpeter Christian Scott aTunde Adjuah.

title: Fats Domino - The North Sea Jazz
subTitle: American singer and pianist Fats Domino gave a performance at the North Sea Jazz Festival of 1980.

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1982, American jazz fusion band Spyro Gyra took the stage at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague; having struck gold with their album "Morning Dance" just a few years prior, they played a slick set with exuberant energy.

title: Schloss Ansbach: Gil Evans & Ornette Coleman
subTitle: Two concerts from Schloss Ansbach in 1978: one by Gil Evans and another by Ornette Coleman.

title: Raul Midon at Singapore 2017
subTitle: Raul Midón appears at 2017's Sing Jazz Festival in Singapore with double bassist Romeir Mendez and drummer Billy Williams.

title: Coleman Hawkins

title: Live in Australia - Louis Armstrong, Billy Kyle, Trummy Young & Aervell Shaw
